title: Reverse auction pilot for forest ecosystem services in rural and peri-urban areas (wildlife population control)
description: A case study evaluating a reverse auction mechanism to finance wildlife population control and habitat restoration in Flanders through the creation of buffer strips between forests and agricultural lands. The aim was to test cost-efficiency and stakeholder acceptance of this innovative business model as an alternative to traditional subsidy schemes, although the pilot failed to attract sufficient bids and incurred high transaction costs.
